title: 10 best herbal tinctures for back pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tinctures helps with back pain because it contains salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin. This compound is absorbed quickly into the bloodstream and works to reduce inflammation and ease muscle tension in the lower back, neck, and spine. As a result, white willow tincture can provide effective relief from chronic pain, acute flare-ups, and stiffness, making it a popular natural remedy for back pain sufferers seeking an alternative or complementary treatment to traditional methods.
remedy_properties: Salicin content: White willow contains salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin that helps reduce pain and inflammation in the body.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The salicin and other bioactive compounds in white willow tincture help reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the muscles and joints associated with back pain.
Analgesic properties: White willow's analgesic properties help to dull or eliminate pain signals sent from the affected area, providing relief from acute and chronic back pain.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: A phenolic glycoside that acts as a natural anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Salicylic acid: A phenolic acid that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate back pain by reducing swelling and inflammation in the affected area.

Quercetin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation.
Buds: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salix alba leaves and twigs when the plant is in full leaf in late spring or early summer.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material at room temperature away from direct sunlight for 2-3 weeks.
Chop the dried plant material into smaller pieces using a sharp knife or a herb cutter.
Combine 1 part dried salix alba material with 2 parts 80% vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:2 ratio.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every few days to facilitate extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and relieve tension in the muscles and joints. The compound glycyrrhizin in licorice root has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, thereby reducing inflammation and discomfort in the back. Additionally, licorice tincture's relaxant properties can help to soothe muscle spasms and improve flexibility, allowing for greater mobility and reduced pain. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to alleviate back pain without relying on pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate back pain caused by conditions such as arthritis or muscle strain.
Antispasmodic effects: The antispasmodic properties of licorice root tincture can help relax muscles and reduce spasms in the back, providing relief from chronic back pain and fibromyalgia.
Hormone regulation: Licorice root has been shown to regulate hormones such as cortisol, which can contribute to back pain caused by hormonal imbalances or adrenal fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Glycosides: These compounds help with back pain by reducing inflammation and pain-causing enzymes, such as COX-2, which contribute to the development of back pain.
Glycyrrhetic acid: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate back pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected areas.

Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which contribute to its pain-relieving effects in back pain by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: These contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the muscles and joints.
Roots: They also contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: Additionally, the roots have been found to have a sedative effect, which can help to relax tense muscles and reduce stress-related pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall when they are mature and dry.
Clean and chop 500 grams of the dried roots into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 1000 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the container every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marijuana tinctures helps with back pain because of their unique ability to target and soothe sore muscles and joints. The potent compounds in these tinctures, such as CBD and THC, work together to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles, providing instant relief from chronic back pain. Additionally, the rapid absorption of marijuana tinctures allows for quick and efficient delivery of therapeutic benefits, making them an effective solution for individuals seeking natural relief from back discomfort.
remedy_properties: Cannabinoids (THC & CBD): Help to reduce inflammation, relieve muscle spasms, and relax tense muscles, which can alleviate back pain by addressing its underlying causes.
Terpenes: Possess analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and sedative properties that help to numb the pain, reduce swelling, and promote relaxation, providing additional relief from back pain.

Essential Fatty Acids (EFAs): Act as natural anti-inflammatories, helping to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common contributor to back pain, and also provide essential nutrients for healthy joint and muscle function.
remedy_constituents: CBD (Cannabidiol): CBD is a non-psychoactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, helping to reduce muscle spasms and inflammation associated with back pain.
CBG (Cannabigerol): CBG is a non-psychoactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, potentially alleviating back pain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Beta-Caryophyllene: Beta-Caryophyllene is a terpene with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help alleviate back pain by reducing inflammation, pain, and muscle spasms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most widely used part for making tinctures due to their high concentration of cannabinoids, particularly THC and CBD, which are known for their pain-relieving properties.

Flowers: They are a popular choice for tinctures because they contain a high percentage of trichomes, which are resinous glands that produce cannabinoids and terpenes, contributing to their analgesic effects.
Buds: They are often used for tinctures due to their high concentration of cannabinoids, particularly THC, which is effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature flowers from cannabis sativa plants when they are dry and free of moisture.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 1 part dried flowers with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean dark glass jar.
Seal the jar and steep it in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking it every day.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with back pain because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The tannins present in maidenhair tree extract are known to reduce inflammation, which is a common underlying cause of back pain. Additionally, the flavonoids and other bioactive compounds in the tincture work together to provide fast and effective relief from back pain, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating discomfort and promoting relaxation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which is often associated with chronic back pain.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels can improve blood flow to the affected area, reducing tension and pressure on the muscles and joints that contribute to back pain.

Neuroprotective compounds: Ginkgo biloba contains neuroprotective compounds that may help reduce nerve irritation and inflammation, which can contribute to chronic back pain by disrupting normal neural function.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are often contributing factors to back pain.

Terpenoids (specifically, Ginkgolides and Bilobalide): These terpenoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate pain and improve mobility in individuals with back pain.
Bilobalide: This terpenoid has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, such as prostaglandins, which contribute to pain and inflammation in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for tinctures due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which are believed to contribute to their potential pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds are also used for tinctures as they contain bioactive compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate back pain.

Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds contain unique compounds, such as bilobalide, which may contribute to their potential pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects, making them a suitable choice for tinctures related to back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in early morning when they are at their highest concentration.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to prevent degradation.
Combine 1 part of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks while shaking the jar every other day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which target the root cause of discomfort. The bioactive compound curcumin in turmeric tinctures reduces inflammation and pain in the muscles and joints, providing relief from backaches. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help to protect the spine from oxidative stress, promoting overall spinal health. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, turmeric tinctures offer a natural solution for alleviating back pain, allowing individuals to enjoy improved mobility and comfort.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric tinctures contain curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the muscles and joints associated with back pain.

Cyclooxygenase inhibition: Turmeric's curcuminoids inhibit the production of cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes, which are responsible for producing prostaglandins that cause pain and inflammation in the body, thereby helping to alleviate back pain symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric tinctures contain antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and damage to tissues, which can contribute to chronic back pain conditions such as degenerative disc disease or sciatica.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps alleviate back pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to muscle and joint pain.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a bioactive curcuminoid, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help mitigate back pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Turmerones (sesquiterpenoids): Turmerones, a class of sesquiterpenoids, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that help reduce back pain by suppressing the activity of inflammatory mediators and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties to help alleviate back pain.
Roots: Containing a high concentration of curcumin and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects.
Buds: Packed with bioactive compounds, including curcumin and other flavonoids, that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with back issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots and wash them thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt.
Chop the turmeric roots into small pieces weighing a total of 500 grams for a standard batch.
Combine the chopped turmeric with 80 percent 95 percent ethanol as a solvent in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth and transfer it to a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica tinctures helps with back pain because it possesses natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herb contains compounds like angelic acid, isoangelicin, and paralytic alpha-santonin that work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the muscles and joints of the lower back. Additionally, angelica has been traditionally used to relax and soothe the nervous system, which can help to reduce tension and stress that often contribute to back pain. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to manage back pain without the use of harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Angelica contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce inflammation, which can contribute to back pain relief by reducing swelling and discomfort.

Muscle relaxants: The volatile oils in angelica, such as bornyl acetate, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to relax tense muscles and alleviate muscle spasms that can exacerbate back pain.
Pain-relieving compounds: Angelica contains analgesic compounds like apiol and bergapten, which have been shown to reduce pain sensitivity and provide relief from back pain.
remedy_constituents: Angelica acid: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate back pain by reducing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues in the muscles and joints.

Ferulic acid: A phenolic compound, ferulic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with back pain, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.

Butyl angelate: An ester compound, butyl angelate has been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to numb pain and reduce swelling in the affected areas, thereby providing relief from back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to alleviate back pain.
Leaves: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties to soothe muscle spasms and ease back pain.
Stems: Employed for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the back area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature angelica archangelica roots and stems in late summer early fall when they are free of moisture.
Clean and chop the angelica archangelica roots and stems into small pieces to enhance extraction.
Combine 250 grams of chopped angelica archangelica with 750 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks to allow for infusion and extraction.
Strain the liquid from the angelica archangelica mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with back pain because it contains a unique combination of compounds that work synergistically to reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and improve circulation. Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain in the muscles and joints, which can be a common source of back pain, and also help to reduce stiffness and improve range of motion.

remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole, a phenolic compound, helps reduce inflammation and relax muscles, thereby alleviating back pain.
Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid, a phenolic acid, acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Trans-anethole: Trans-anethole, a terpene, exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relieve pain and discomfort in the back.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are rich in essential oils, particularly anethole, which is responsible for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving back pain.
Leaves: The leaves contain compounds like estragole and limonene, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to soothe back pain and reduce inflammation.

Stems: The stems of Foeniculum vulgare contain a mixture of essential oils, including anethole, which provides pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for back pain tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves when mature and dry them thoroughly in a warm dark place.
Chop 500 grams of the dried plant material into small pieces and combine with 750 milliliters of 80 proof vodka.
Steep the plant-vodka mixture in a glass container in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.
Store the foeniculum vulgare tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before use to mature the flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with back pain because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation and relax muscles. The tincture's natural analgesic properties also help to block pain signals to the brain, providing instant relief from backaches caused by strain or injury. Nerve soothing properties: The triterpenoid saponins present in stinging nettle tincture have been shown to have a calming effect on nerve endings, which can help to reduce numbness, tingling, and sciatic pain associated with back issues.

remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Urtica dioica tinctures help with back pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which contribute to pain and discomfort in the muscles and joints.

Naphthoquinones (Plumbagin): Naphthoquinones, particularly plumbagin, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate back pain by reducing pain transmission and inflammation in the affected area.

Stylopine (Alkaloid): Stylopine, an alkaloid present in Urtica dioica, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help relieve back pain by reducing pain perception and inflammation in the muscles and joints.

Roots: The roots contain glycosides and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate back pain.
Stems: The stems contain rutoside and other flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the back.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems by cutting them with scissors at dawn when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested urtica dioica plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried urtica dioica plant material with 2 parts 80-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking of the jar.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

Buds: Contain phenolic compounds, which may have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh rosa rugosa flowers and leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Combine the harvested rosa rugosa flowers and leaves with 60% glycerin in a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for 2 to 3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry tinctures helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated muscles and joints. The tannins present in highbush cranberries have a natural analgesic effect, reducing swelling and discomfort. Additionally, the antioxidants found in the tincture help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, promoting healing and relaxation. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Highbush cranberry contains anti-inflammatory compounds like anthocyanins and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating back pain caused by conditions like arthritis or muscle strain.

Antispasmodic properties: The tannins present in highbush cranberry have antispasmodic effects, helping to relax muscles and ease spasms that can contribute to back pain, such as tension headaches or sciatica.

Oxidative stress reduction: Highbush cranberry's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and free radical damage in the body, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and pain, including back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the highbush cranberry plant contain a compound called berberine, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.

remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help alleviate back pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with muscle strain and injury.
Quercetin: A flavonoid with potent anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin helps reduce pain and inflammation in the muscles and joints, providing relief from back pain.

Proanthocyanidins: These compounds have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing pain and inflammation in the muscles and joints, contributing to back pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of vaccinium macrocarpon berries when ripe in late summer.
Dry the vaccinium macrocarpon berries in a single layer at 80 degrees fahrenheit for 2 weeks.
Grind 1 cup of dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Steep 1 part of the ground vaccinium macrocarpon powder in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and store it in a glass bottle in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with back pain because it offers a natural, non-invasive solution for relief. The menthol in peppermint has a calming effect on muscles, reducing inflammation and spasms that contribute to back pain. Additionally, the cooling sensation of peppermint tincture can help ease stiffness and numbness associated with chronic pain conditions. When applied topically or taken internally, herbal peppermint tinctures provide rapid relief from discomfort, allowing individuals to regain mobility and enjoy improved overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a key compound found in peppermint oil, helps to numb and reduce pain by blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing relief from back pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the muscles and joints, which can contribute to back pain.

Relaxation and muscle relaxation: The menthol and other compounds in peppermint tinctures can help to relax tense muscles, relieve spasms, and reduce muscle cramping, all of which can contribute to back pain relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other bioactive compounds, peppermint roots are often used to create a powerful analgesic tincture that targets muscle and joint pain.
Rhyzomes: With their high concentration of volatile oils, peppermint rhyzomes are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve back pain associated with conditions like sciatica.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a well-known organic source ensuring quality control.
Weigh out one part of dried or two parts of fresh peppermint leaves using a precise scale.
Combine the measured peppermint leaves with 60% ethyl alcohol in a glass jar leaving minimal headspace.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks macerating slowly.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with back pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The tincture's active compounds, including linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated muscles, providing relief from chronic pain and discomfort. Additionally, the calming effects of English lavender promote relaxation, which can help to reduce muscle tension and alleviate stress, common contributors to back pain. remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties: English Lavender tincture has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation, making it a natural remedy for alleviating back pain caused by muscle strain or injury.

Calming and sedative properties: The calming effects of English Lavender tincture can help to relax tense muscles, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote better sleep, all of which can contribute to reducing back pain symptoms.

Antispasmodic properties: English Lavender tincture has been found to relax muscle spasms and cramps, which are common contributors to back pain, and can help to provide relief from chronic lower back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots contain a high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which are believed to help reduce inflammation and relax muscles in the back.

Barks: The bark of the lavender plant contains a unique combination of terpenes and phenolic compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective for soothing back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn when the dew has dried off.
Chop the flowers coarsely to release their essential oils and then dry them in a single layer.
Steep one part dried lavender flowers in two parts high-proof vodka for four weeks in a clean glass jar.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids.
Transfer the clear liquid to dark glass bottles and store in a cool dark place for six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The gel inside the aloe leaf contains powerful compounds that work to reduce inflammation and relieve muscle tension, which can be a major contributor to chronic back pain. As a result, many people find that using herbal aloe tinctures as part of their daily routine helps them manage their back pain and enjoy greater overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which helps to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with back pain.
Gel-like mucilage: The gel-like substance in aloe vera has been shown to soothe and protect the tissues of the spine, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by back pain.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to chronic back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, aloe roots are used to treat chronic back pain due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: The gel inside aloe leaves contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as amino acids, which help soothe and calm back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera leaves from mature plants for optimal medicinal properties.
Clean and chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces for efficient extraction.
Steep the chopped aloe vera leaves in a mixture of 80 proof vodka and water for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to separate the tincture from solids.
Bottle the prepared tincture and store it in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with back pain because its unique combination of active compounds, including kavain and dihydrokavain, targets the root causes of discomfort. These natural substances have a sedative effect on the nervous system, calming muscle spasms and reducing inflammation in the affected areas. Additionally, kava's anxiolytic properties help alleviate stress-related tension that can exacerbate back pain. By addressing both physical and emotional factors, herbal kava tinctures offer a holistic approach to managing back discomfort, promoting relaxation and relief from chronic pain.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Kava's anxiolytic effects can help reduce muscle tension and spasms in the back, which are common contributors to back pain.

Pain-relieving properties: Kava contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body, such as bradykinin and substance P, helping to alleviate back pain.
Muscle relaxant properties: Kava's muscle relaxant effects can help reduce muscle stiffness and spasms in the back, allowing for improved flexibility and reduced pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of medicinal compounds in kava, containing a high concentration of kavalactones that have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for relieving back pain.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes, which are underground stems that connect roots, contain a higher concentration of kavalactones than the roots themselves, and their use in tinctures can provide an intense therapeutic effect for alleviating back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh kava root from trusted sources in amounts sufficient for processing.
Cut the harvested roots into small pieces and dry them thoroughly using air circulation or dehydrator.
Combine one part dried kava root with 5 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar for extraction.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate solvent penetration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into another clean container discarding solids and storing in dark glass bottles.

As a result, herbal yellow jessamine tinctures have been traditionally used to soothe sore muscles and joints, providing effective relief from chronic back pain.
remedy_properties: Muscle Relaxant Properties: Yellow Jessamine's tincture has been shown to relax muscles, reducing muscle spasms and cramps in the back, which can contribute to chronic pain.
Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the back muscles and joints, which can exacerbate back pain.

Antioxidant Properties: Yellow Jessamine's antioxidant properties may help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic back pain and degenerative conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a wealth of bioactive compounds, roots are prized for their ability to alleviate muscle and joint pain associated with back discomfort.
Barks: The barks' high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids makes them an effective ingredient in tinctures aimed at reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gelsemium sempervirens root and stem material early in the morning on a dry sunny day.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and combine it with high-proof vodka at a ratio of 1 part plant material to 5 parts vodka.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass jar for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the plant material to infuse its active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid tincture.
Store the finished tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 2 years, shaking well before use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with back pain because of its unique ability to soothe and calm inflamed joints and muscles. The mucilages present in marshmallow root create a protective barrier that reduces inflammation and discomfort, allowing for improved mobility and flexibility. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow help to reduce pressure on the spine, alleviating tension and strain on the back muscles. As a result, herbal marshmallow tinctures can provide significant relief from chronic back pain, promoting overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow root contains high amounts of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that coats and soothes the digestive tract and joints, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with back pain.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the muscles and joints of the back.

Softpaste-like properties: Marshmallow root has a softpasty texture that helps to lubricate and cushion the spine, reducing friction and pressure on the vertebrae and discs, which can contribute to back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain mucilage, a thickening agent that helps soothe and lubricate the joints and muscles, relieving back pain.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the back, alleviating pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried marshmallow roots and leaves according to the desired dosage.
Clean and chop the harvested plant material into small pieces for efficient extraction.
Combine one part of chopped althaea officinalis with two parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 4-6 weeks of preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with back pain because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that target specific areas of discomfort, reducing muscle spasms and joint stiffness. The herbal remedy also exhibits analgesic properties, soothing irritated nerves and dulling pain signals to the brain. Additionally, its antioxidant effects help to reduce inflammation and promote overall healing in the affected area, leading to improved flexibility and range of motion. As a result, purple coneflower tinctures can provide natural relief from back pain, allowing for a better quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid and sesquiterpenes, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate back pain caused by conditions such as arthritis or degenerative disc disease.

Analgesic properties: The tincture of purple coneflower has been shown to have analgesic (pain-relieving) effects, possibly due to its ability to inhibit the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body, making it a useful natural remedy for back pain management.

Muscle relaxant compounds: Purple coneflower contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which is particularly beneficial for people experiencing back pain caused by strained or overused muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in mid-summer for optimal medicinal properties.
Clean and dry the harvested material by rinsing with water and air-drying for two days.
Chop the dried roots and flowers into small pieces and combine them in a glass jar in a 4:1 ratio.
Cover the jar with vodka and store it at room temperature for six weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense tinctures helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The resin of the Boswellia plant, from which frankincense is derived, contains compounds that reduce inflammation and swelling in the muscles and joints. This can help alleviate back pain caused by conditions such as arthritis, herniated discs, or strained muscles. Additionally, frankincense tinctures have been shown to relax the muscles and improve range of motion, further reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense tincture contains boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and relieve back pain caused by inflammation.

Pain relief: Frankincense contains incensole acetate, a natural analgesic that has been shown to have a similar effect as pharmaceutical painkillers, providing quick and effective relief from back pain.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory antioxidant activities: Frankincense tincture has been found to possess antioxidant properties, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by free radicals, contributing to chronic back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, the roots of the frankincense plant are prized for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal tinctures for back pain relief.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of frankincense contain high levels of boswellic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve joint and muscle pain associated with back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried boswellia serrata roots and cut them into small pieces weighing approximately 500 grams.
Combine the chopped boswellia serrata with 70% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar covering the plant material completely.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing for infusion and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids after pressing out as much liquid as possible.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles filling them to the top and storing them in a cool dark place for up to two years.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for back pain are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate chronic back pain by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and improving circulation. The best herbal tinctures for back pain include Zingiber officinale, Curcuma longa, Cannabis sativa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Arnica montana, Capsicum annuum, Valeriana officinalis, Salix alba, Achillea millefolium, and Salvia miltiorrhiza. If left untreated, back pain can lead to decreased mobility, fatigue, and decreased quality of life, making everyday activities, such as work and social interactions, increasingly challenging.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to back pain that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions such as arthritis, fibromyalgia, and sciatica. These herbal remedies help alleviate symptoms of inflammation, stiffness, and discomfort associated with these conditions. For example, turmeric-based tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties that may reduce joint pain and swelling, while willow bark tinctures contain salicin, a natural pain reliever similar to aspirin. Other herbs like ginger and cayenne pepper may also provide relief from muscle and joint discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with back pain is a blend of Willow Bark, Turmeric, and Ginger. Willow Bark contains salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory that reduces pain and inflammation. Turmeric's Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the body. Ginger adds warmth and reduces muscle spasms, promoting relaxation and flexibility. Together, these tinctures work synergistically to alleviate back pain, promoting healing and well-being. This blend can be taken sublingually or added to warm water for a soothing tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease muscle tension in the back. The active compound, gingerol, is known to inhibit the production of pain-causing chemicals, such as prostaglandins. Additionally, ginger's natural warming effect helps increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting relaxation and reducing stiffness. This combination of anti-inflammatory and warming properties makes Zingiber officinale tinctures an effective natural remedy for alleviating back pain and promoting overall spinal health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Helps with back pain by reducing inflammation and relieving pain through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Shogaol: Contributes to alleviating back pain by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body and reducing inflammation.
6-Gingerol: Plays a role in back pain relief by displaying potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the musculoskeletal system.
remedy_parts: Buds: Ginger buds, which are the young shoots of the plant, are sometimes used in tinctures for their high concentration of volatile oils, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in late summer or early fall when they are at their peak potency.
Clean and chop the rhizomes into small pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine the dried zingiber officinale pieces with 80 percent vodka and 20 percent filtered water in a large glass jar at a ratio of 1 part plant material to 2 parts solvent.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids then bottle the tincture and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tinctures helps with back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the affected areas. The curcumin present in Curcuma longa tinctures inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus providing relief from chronic back pain. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to protect the muscles and joints from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall health and well-being. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate back pain and improve mobility.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It reduces inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby alleviating back pain.
Volatile oils (including turmerone and atlantone): They possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Demethoxycurcumin: It has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the back muscles, thereby alleviating pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Also contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds that contribute to their pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: While less commonly used, turmeric leaves are also known to contain curcumin and other compounds beneficial for pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried curcuma longa roots and 2 parts of a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin.
Combine 1 cup of the solvent with 2 ounces of the dried roots in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and let it steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years or until it is needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cannabis sativa tinctures helps with back pain because the body's endocannabinoid system plays a crucial role in pain management. The tinctures contain compounds like THC and CBD, which interact with the body's cannabinoid receptors, reducing inflammation and relieving pain. This can help alleviate back pain caused by conditions such as arthritis, muscle strain, or spinal injuries. As the tinctures take effect, individuals often report a significant decrease in pain, improved mobility, and enhanced overall well-being. Regular use may also promote long-term pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Cannabidiol (CBD): Reduces inflammation and pain by interacting with the body's endocannabinoid system, providing relief from back pain and discomfort.
Cannabigerol (CBG): Exhibits potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate back pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area.
Beta-Caryophyllene (β-Caryophyllene): A non-psychoactive terpene with potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain a high concentration of cannabinoids, particularly THC and CBD, which are effective in relieving pain and inflammation.
Leaves: They also contain cannabinoids and have been traditionally used to make tinctures for pain relief, with some studies suggesting their potential in reducing inflammation and pain.
Buds: Similar to flowers, buds are rich in cannabinoids and are often used to make tinctures for pain management due to their high THC and CBD content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cannabis sativa flowers and leaves to be used for making herbal tincture.
Combine 1 cup of harvested flowers and leaves with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and place it in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to infuse the vodka.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container to remove solids.
Store the herbal tincture in a glass dropper bottle and label it for future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the muscles and joints. The licorice root extract in Glycyrrhiza glabra has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing pain and inflammation. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has been found to have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in relieving muscle cramps and spasms that can contribute to back pain. Its natural compounds work to soothe and calm the affected areas, providing relief from back pain.
remedy_constituents: Glycosides: They help with back pain by reducing inflammation and relieving pain through their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Terpenoids: They alleviate back pain by exerting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and pain-relieving effects, which contribute to the reduction of muscle spasms and inflammation.

Flavonoids (specifically, liquiritin): They contribute to pain relief by modulating the body's pain response, reducing inflammation, and acting as antioxidants to minimize oxidative stress that can exacerbate back pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate back pain.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used to make tinctures for back pain as they contain flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures for back pain due to their rich content of glycosides and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root from a trusted supplier or cultivate it in a controlled environment.
Combine the root with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass container and stir well.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks or 14 days with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container to remove the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle and keep it in a cool dry place after filtering and pressing out excess liquid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana tinctures helps with back pain because it contains compounds that reduce inflammation and promote healing. The anti-inflammatory properties in Arnica help to relax tense muscles and calm irritated nerves, providing relief from back pain. Additionally, Arnica's regenerative properties stimulate the body's natural healing process, which can help repair damaged tissues and improve overall back health. By using Arnica montana tinctures, individuals can potentially experience a reduction in back pain and an improvement in their overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Helenalin: Acts as a potent anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain in the muscles and joints associated with back pain.
Palustrene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort caused by back strain or injury.
Inuline-derived inulin oligosaccharides: Possesses anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative properties, which may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected areas of the back.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for the anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate back pain.
Leaves: They also contain sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to the anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects of the tincture.
Roots: They are rich in sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relieve back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1:1 ratio of fresh arnica montana flowers to 80-proof vodka by hand in the morning.
Dry the gathered flowers in a single layer at 25c for 2 to 3 weeks in a well-ventilated area.
Combine the dried arnica montana flowers with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a 1:5 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking and inversion.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum tinctures helps with back pain because it contains capsaicin, a compound that blocks the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. By inhibiting the release of substance P, capsaicin reduces inflammation and relaxes muscle spasms, providing relief from back pain. This natural anti-inflammatory effect also improves blood flow, reducing stiffness and promoting healing in the affected area, making Capsicum annuum tinctures a popular natural remedy for back pain sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin helps with back pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing pain perception.
Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid helps with back pain by reducing inflammation, which is a common contributing factor to back pain, and also by acting as an antioxidant to prevent oxidative stress.
Piperine: Piperine helps with back pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing pain transmission, thus providing relief from back pain.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Capsicum annuum fruits, commonly known as peppers or chili peppers, are used to make tinctures due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Capsicum annuum leaves are used to make tinctures because they contain capsaicin, a compound that helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the body.
Seeds: Capsicum annuum seeds are used to make tinctures for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate back pain and improve overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of mature capsicum annuum fruit at peak ripeness to ensure optimal potency.
Chop the harvested fruit into small pieces using sharp scissors or a knife to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped capsicum annuum fruit with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and steep for 2-3 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tinctures helps with back pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which effectively reduce muscle tension and relax the body. The active compounds in Valerian root, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, interact with the nervous system to promote relaxation and reduce pain perception. This natural remedy also helps to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by chronic back pain, further alleviating symptoms and promoting overall well-being. Regular use can lead to sustained relief.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Valeranone: Valeranone is a potent muscle relaxant that helps to reduce muscle spasms and cramps, providing relief from back pain caused by muscle tension.
Isovaleramide: Isovaleramide has sedative and anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate back pain, promoting relaxation and pain relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Contain valerenic acid and other compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation.
Leaves: Have a high concentration of valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that soothe back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes in the fall when the plant is mature and the foliage has died back.
Clean the valeriana officinalis plant parts in cold running water and then dry them thoroughly with paper towels.
Use a glass jar and add 1000 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots and rhizomes to the jar.
Pour 750 milliliters of 80-proof vodka over the valeriana officinalis plant parts and seal the jar tightly.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks and then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salix alba tinctures helps with back pain because it contains salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and relieves pain. The tincture also has analgesic properties, making it effective in soothing back pain. Additionally, Salix alba has been used in traditional medicine to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate muscle tension and spasms that contribute to back pain. By using Salix alba tincture, individuals can experience natural relief from back pain.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: A salicylate compound that helps reduce inflammation and pain in the back by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are mediators of inflammation and pain.
Flavonoids: A group of plant-based compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the back muscles and joints.
Phenolic acids: Compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the back muscles and joints, contributing to pain relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used as they contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides pain relief and reduces inflammation.
Stems: The stems are used for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to ease back pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of salix alba bark and 500ml of high-proof vodka or ethanol for tincture.
Combine the salix alba bark with 500ml of vodka in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and allow it to steep for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Label and date the tincture and store it in a dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tinctures helps with back pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The tincture contains compounds such as achillenol and sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the muscles and joints. Additionally, its antispasmodic properties help to relax tense muscles and improve blood flow, reducing discomfort and stiffness in the back. This natural remedy provides relief from chronic back pain, promoting relaxation and overall well-being. It also supports the body's natural healing process.
remedy_constituents: Cichoriin: Cichoriin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with back pain.
Apigenin: Apigenin has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort in the back.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation in the back and joints.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Stems: The stems are used for their rich content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them effective for back pain relief.
Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, helping to alleviate back pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has dried for better potency.
Chop the fresh plant material into smaller pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 100 grams of chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks with occasional shaking to aid extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza tinctures helps with back pain because it is rich in compounds called salvianolic acids and tanshinones, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the muscles and joints, alleviating back pain and discomfort. Additionally, Salvia miltiorrhiza has been shown to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the affected areas, which can help to reduce pain and promote recovery. This makes it a popular natural remedy for back pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Tanshinones: Tanshinones help with back pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to pain and discomfort.
Ligustilide: Ligustilide helps with back pain by exhibiting analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relieve pain and reduce swelling in the affected area.
Cryptotanshinone: Cryptotanshinone helps with back pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which can contribute to pain and inflammation in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Salvia miltiorrhiza are primarily used due to their high concentration of active compounds, such as tanshinones, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Stems: The stems of Salvia miltiorrhiza are utilized for their similar content of tanshinones, which contribute to the plant's analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: The leaves of Salvia miltiorrhiza are used as they contain a significant amount of phenolic acids and flavonoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh salvia miltiorrhiza roots and rinse them thoroughly with cold water.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine them with 500 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow the roots to infuse into the solvent.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
